title: echium italicum subsp. pyrenaicum
intro: Echium italicum subsp. pyrenaicum, also known as the Pyrenean viper's bugloss, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support digestive health. Echium italicum subsp. pyrenaicum can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions for use in traditional medicine, although its effectiveness may vary depending on the individual and the specific preparation method.
